steps.title: Use the perimeter formula
steps.content: A rectangle has two equal lengths and two equal widths. The perimeter is the distance around all four sides, so add two lengths and two widths.

steps.title: Substitute the side lengths
steps.content: Use $l=15$ and $w=7$ in the perimeter formula. $$P=2(15)+2(7)$$ $$P=30+14$$ $$P=44$$ The rectangle's perimeter is $44$, which is the requested value. The answer is C.
